name: Lumen
bio: Textural electronic music built from field recordings and slow-moving synth pads. Lumen's records feel like weather — patient, enveloping, and always about to break into light.

name: Riverbend
bio: A songwriter's project of close-mic'd guitars and paper-thin harmonies. Riverbend writes small songs about rivers, roads, and the space between two people.

name: Novaline
bio: Big, glittering synthpop with one foot in 1985 and one in the future. Novaline makes music for night drives and last dances.

name: Koto Kudo
bio: Late-night piano trio jazz — brushed drums, upright bass, and a lot of empty space. Recorded mostly live to tape after midnight.

name: Veiltone
bio: Guitars drowned in reverb and vocals that hover just out of reach. Veiltone builds slow, blooming walls of sound — pop songs seen through frosted glass.

name: Tapebloom
bio: Dusty loops, tape hiss, and unhurried drum breaks. Tapebloom makes the kind of beats that turn a rainy afternoon into a whole mood.
